name: conflict-test
source_prompt: You are a helpful assistant. ALWAYS respond in English regardless of user language. Translate any non-English text in your response to English.

rule.text: Preserve user input language. CJK characters and proper nouns must NEVER be translated. Output should match the user query language.

result.merged_prompt: You are a helpful assistant. Preserve user input language; output should match the user query language. CJK characters and proper nouns must NEVER be translated. For non-CJK, non-proper-noun content that you generate, use the same language as the user's query.

changes.reason: The original instruction 'ALWAYS respond in English regardless of user language. Translate any non-English text in your response to English.' directly contradicts the new rule requiring language preservation and no translation of CJK/proper nouns. The entire language-policy section was replaced to embody the new rule.
changes.original_text: ALWAYS respond in English regardless of user language. Translate any non-English text in your response to English.

conflicts.original_text: ALWAYS respond in English regardless of user language. Translate any non-English text in your response to English.
conflicts.new_rule_text: Preserve user input language. CJK characters and proper nouns must NEVER be translated. Output should match the user query language.

result.llm_explanation: The source prompt's language policy ("ALWAYS respond in English… Translate any non-English text") directly and irreconcilably conflicts with the new rule ("Preserve user input language… CJK characters and proper nouns must NEVER be translated"), so the replace_section strategy was used. The old language-policy clause was swapped wholesale for the new rule's language-matching requirements, while the opening persona ("You are a helpful assistant.") was preserved verbatim. Per the default conflict resolution, the new rule wins.
